raw_input: Write an apology email (180–220 words) from Jordan Takahashi, CEO of a SaaS company called 'Flint', to enterprise customers after a 6-hour production outage on 16 April 2026. The outage was caused by a bad database migration. Structure: (1) open with a concrete acknowledgement of the impact — not generic 'we experienced an outage'; (2) explain what went wrong in one sentence; (3) state the two corrective actions the team is taking (rolling back the migration framework to the prior version, and adding a mandatory staging-replay step before all future migrations); (4) offer a goodwill credit of 10% of April charges; (5) sign off as 'Jordan Takahashi, CEO, Flint'. Tone: accountable, not defensive. Avoid hollow phrases.
success_criteria: mentions the specific outage date (16 April) and duration (6 hours)
constraints: must end with sign-off containing 'Jordan Takahashi'
no phrase 'we take this very seriously'
